qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Improper unified lifetime in ssl::select_next_proto enables use-after-free
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies a lifetime annotation bug in ssl::select_next_proto() at openssl/src/ssl/mod.rs:701 that causes use-after-free. The advisory describes a missing length check in rustMdCtxRef::digest_final() that causes buffer overflow. Both vulnerabilities are in rust-openssl but involve different functions (select_next_proto vs digest_final), different root causes (incorrect lifetime annotation vs missing length check), and different vulnerability classes (use-after-free vs stack buffer overflow).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes a stack buffer overflow in rustMdCtxRef::digest_final() caused by a missing length check before EVP_DigestFinal() writes EVP_MD_CTX_size(ctx) bytes. The select_next_proto_lifetime_uaf finding identifies a different vulnerability in the same repository (rust-openssl) but in a different function (ssl::select_next_proto) with a different root cause (incorrect unified lifetime annotation leading to use-after-free).
evidence: Advisory identifies vulnerable function: rustMdCtxRef::digest_final() in openssl/src/md.rs
Advisory root cause: Missing length check before EVP_DigestFinal() writes EVP_MD_CTX_size(ctx) bytes to caller buffer
Advisory vulnerability class: CWE-121 Stack-based Buffer Overflow
Finding select_next_proto_lifetime_uaf identifies vulnerable function: ssl::select_next_proto() at openssl/src/ssl/mod.rs:701
Finding select_next_proto_lifetime_uaf root cause: Unified lifetime annotation 'a on both parameters when output only depends on server buffer
Finding select_next_proto_lifetime_uaf vulnerability class: Use-after-free (lifetime soundness violation)
Finding does not reference digest_final, MdCtxRef, or EVP_DigestFinal
